name: AWS API (uvx)
description: Interact with AWS services and resources by running AWS CLI commands through natural language. Includes built-in command validation, security controls, and an optional read-only mode. Authenticates with standard AWS credentials.

inputs.label: AWS Access Key ID
inputs.description: The access key ID for an AWS IAM user or role. Used together with the secret access key to authenticate AWS CLI commands. Grant only the permissions you want the server to be able to use.

obtain.instructions:
1. Sign in to the AWS Console and open the IAM service
2. Go to Users and select (or create) the IAM user you want to use
3. Open the 'Security credentials' tab
4. Under 'Access keys', click 'Create access key'
5. Copy the Access key ID shown

inputs.label: AWS Secret Access Key
inputs.description: The secret access key paired with the access key ID. Shown only once when the access key is created. Keep it secret; it is stored encrypted in your OS keychain.

obtain.instructions:
1. In the IAM Console, open the user's 'Security credentials' tab
2. Click 'Create access key' (or use an existing one)
3. Copy the 'Secret access key' shown at creation time
4. You cannot retrieve it again later, so store it now

inputs.label: AWS Region
inputs.description: The default AWS region for CLI commands when a request does not specify one (for example us-east-1, eu-west-1, ap-southeast-2).

auth.instructions: Authenticates with standard AWS credentials. Create an access key for an IAM user or role in the AWS IAM Console (Security credentials > Access keys), then provide the Access Key ID, Secret Access Key, and a default region.
